ingredients.item:
BECHAMEL SAUCE
¾ cup (180 g) unsalted butter
¾ cup (100 g) all-purpose flour
ingredients.original:
BECHAMEL SAUCE
¾ cup (180 g) unsalted butter
¾ cup (100 g) all-purpose flour

ingredients.item: 3 cups plus 2 tablespoons (750 ml) whole milk
ingredients.original: 3 cups plus 2 tablespoons (750 ml) whole milk

ingredients.item: 2 TO MAKE THE BEEF SAUCE AND PASTA: In a large, 2-inch (5 cm) deep, oven-safe skillet, heat the oil over medium heat. Add the onion and cook, stirring often, until softened, 5 minutes. Add the ground beef and cook, breaking it up into small pieces with a wooden spoon, for 5 to 7 minutes, until browned and the liquid has released. Add 2½ teaspoons of the salt, the allspice, cinnamon, 1 teaspoon pepper, onion powder, garlic powder, and passata and cook for 5 to 7 more minutes.
ingredients.original: 2 TO MAKE THE BEEF SAUCE AND PASTA: In a large, 2-inch (5 cm) deep, oven-safe skillet, heat the oil over medium heat. Add the onion and cook, stirring often, until softened, 5 minutes. Add the ground beef and cook, breaking it up into small pieces with a wooden spoon, for 5 to 7 minutes, until browned and the liquid has released. Add 2½ teaspoons of the salt, the allspice, cinnamon, 1 teaspoon pepper, onion powder, garlic powder, and passata and cook for 5 to 7 more minutes.

ingredients.item: 3 Add the uncooked pasta to the pan along with the boiling water and the remaining ½ teaspoon salt. Stir to combine, cover with the lid, and cook for 15 minutes, stirring every 5 minutes or so to prevent the pasta from sticking to the skillet. If the pasta still has a bit of a bite, that is fine as it will continue cooking in the oven. Turn the heat off.
ingredients.original: 3 Add the uncooked pasta to the pan along with the boiling water and the remaining ½ teaspoon salt. Stir to combine, cover with the lid, and cook for 15 minutes, stirring every 5 minutes or so to prevent the pasta from sticking to the skillet. If the pasta still has a bit of a bite, that is fine as it will continue cooking in the oven. Turn the heat off.

ingredients.item: 4 MEANWHILE, MAKE THE BECHAMEL SAUCE: In a medium saucepan, melt the butter over medium-low heat. Add the flour and whisk vigorously for 4 to 5 minutes, until the flour absorbs all the butter and looks dry. Add the milk gradually and continue whisking the bechamel for 8 to 10 minutes, until it starts to thicken. Stir in the 1½ teaspoons salt, ½ teaspoon pepper, and nutmeg. Dip a spoon into the bechamel, then run your finger down the spoon’s back; if the sauce separates, it is ready.
ingredients.original: 4 MEANWHILE, MAKE THE BECHAMEL SAUCE: In a medium saucepan, melt the butter over medium-low heat. Add the flour and whisk vigorously for 4 to 5 minutes, until the flour absorbs all the butter and looks dry. Add the milk gradually and continue whisking the bechamel for 8 to 10 minutes, until it starts to thicken. Stir in the 1½ teaspoons salt, ½ teaspoon pepper, and nutmeg. Dip a spoon into the bechamel, then run your finger down the spoon’s back; if the sauce separates, it is ready.

ingredients.item: 5 Mix the pasta and beef sauce one more time and use a spoon to flatten its surface. Slowly pour the bechamel on top, spreading it evenly.
ingredients.original: 5 Mix the pasta and beef sauce one more time and use a spoon to flatten its surface. Slowly pour the bechamel on top, spreading it evenly.

ingredients.item: 6 Bake, uncovered, for 20 to 25 minutes. If the sauce starts to leak to the top and around the edges, that’s normal. Broil the top for 3 to 4 minutes, until golden, then serve immediately.
ingredients.original: 6 Bake, uncovered, for 20 to 25 minutes. If the sauce starts to leak to the top and around the edges, that’s normal. Broil the top for 3 to 4 minutes, until golden, then serve immediately.
